race.name: La Sgambeda
race.tagline: Thirty kilometers at 1,800 meters in the December thin air of Livigno — the season opener where the Ski Classics world shakes off the rust on Italy's highest-altitude racing stage.

vitals.aid_stations: Feed stations along the high-altitude course

climate.description: December at 1,800m altitude in Livigno — one of the coldest valleys in the Alps. Reliable early-season snow thanks to extreme altitude. Temperatures typically -15°C to -5°C. The enclosed valley traps cold air, making Livigno consistently one of the coldest inhabited places in the Alps.
challenges: Racing at 1,800m altitude — reduced oxygen demands acclimatization
December cold in one of the Alps' coldest valleys
Early-season fitness — first race of the year for many
Altitude effects compound the physical demands of freestyle technique

course.primary: Freestyle course in the Livigno valley at 1,800m altitude, making it one of the highest-altitude major ski marathons in Europe. The course follows trails in and around the duty-free resort town. Livigno's flat valley floor provides fast skating terrain with minimal technical difficulty, but the altitude is the defining challenge.
course.surface: Machine-groomed freestyle tracks at high altitude
course.grooming: Excellent — Livigno invests heavily in early-season grooming, Ski Classics Challengers standard
features: 1,800m altitude — one of the highest ski marathons in Europe
Season opener for the Ski Classics circuit
Livigno — duty-free Alpine resort with excellent infrastructure
Guaranteed early-season snow thanks to extreme altitude
Italian hospitality in a world-class resort setting

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Maximum altitude and scenery scores — 1,800m in an Alpine setting. Top-tier snow reliability at this altitude in December. Strong international draw as Ski Classics season opener. Competitive depth elevated by pro-circuit presence. Shorter 30km distance limits the distance score.
nordic_lab_rating.tier_note: Score 69 >= 60 qualifies for TIER 2. Prestige 3 — no override.

history.summary: Founded around 1990, La Sgambeda is a 30km freestyle race in Livigno, Italy, at 1,800m altitude. It serves as the traditional season opener for the Ski Classics Challengers series, held in December when few other European venues can guarantee snow. Livigno's extreme altitude and cold valley climate make it one of the earliest reliable snow venues in Europe. The name 'La Sgambeda' is Lombard dialect meaning roughly 'the leg-stretcher.'
notable_facts: Season opener for the Ski Classics circuit — first distance race of the winter
1,800m altitude makes it one of Europe's highest ski marathons
Livigno is a duty-free zone — unique in the European Alps
Livigno was chosen as a venue for the 2026 Milan-Cortina Winter Olympics (biathlon/XC)
Part of the Ski Classics Challengers series

videos.title: La Sgambeda 2012
videos.description:
La stagione invernale di Livigno apre i battenti con La Sgambeda, la prima granfondo del circuito FIS Marathon Cup. 
Un appuntamento di 42 km per i più allenati o di 22,5 per quanti invece vogliono testare le loro  condizioni fisiche a inizio stagione.

#Sgambeda 2015: 4 gare in 2 giorni, per un super mix di sport e divertimento! #Livigno

#Sgambeda 2015: 4 races in 2 days, for a great mix of sport and fun! Ski Classics​

videos.title: Livigno 15 dicembre 2024 - partenza della Sgambeda - gara di sci di fondo
videos.description:
Livigno: anche quest’anno si è svolta la “Sgambeda”, una gara di sci di fondo molto conosciuta. Solitamente di 35 km, è stata accorciata a 25 km a causa della scarsità di neve sul percorso. Nonostante tutto, un grande lavoro ha reso possibile la manifestazione.
Vi mostro la partenza e la prima parte del percorso❄️

Livigno: the "Sgambeda", a well-known cross-country ski race, was held again this year. Usually 35 km, it was shortened to 25 km due to the lack of snow on the route. Despite everything, a great work made the event possible.
Here is the start and the first part of the route❄️

#livigno #livignolive #crosscountryskiing #lasgambeda
videos.curation_reason: Most recent video (2024) showing race start and course details with specific information about course conditions and snow challenges.
